questions.q: A company issued 10,000, 12% debentures of Rs. 100 each. The company can be said to have incurred debenture redemption reserve when the amount of debentures redeemed exceeds the amount of profit and loss account.
What is the difference between a premium on issue of debentures and a discount on issue of debentures?
questions.answer: A premium on issue of debentures is the excess of face value of debentures over their issue value, whereas a discount on issue of debentures is the excess of issue value of debentures over their face value.

questions.q: A company has outstanding 5,000, 10% debentures of Rs. 100 each. Calculate the amount of interest on debentures for the year ending 31st March.
What is meant by debenture redemption reserve? Give its purpose.
questions.answer: Debenture redemption reserve is a provision made by the company to write-off the premium on issue of debentures and to meet the redemption of debentures. It is created when the debentures are issued at a premium.

questions.q: A company issued 12% debentures of Rs. 100 each. The company can pay interest on debentures half-yearly. Calculate the interest on debentures for the year ending 31st March.
The amount of interest on debentures is credited to which of the following accounts?
State the difference between redeemable and irredeemable debentures.
questions.answer: Redeemable debentures are those which are to be redeemed at a future date, whereas irredeemable debentures are those which are not to be redeemed at any future date.

questions.q: A company issued 5,000, 10% debentures of Rs. 100 each at a premium of 10%. Calculate the amount of premium on issue of debentures.
State the main advantage of debentures.
questions.answer: The main advantage of debentures is that they provide a long-term source of finance to the company.

questions.q: What is the difference between debenture and share capital?
questions.answer: Debenture is a type of loan capital, whereas share capital is a type of equity capital. Debentures are secured by a charge on the assets of the company, whereas shares are not secured.
